title: Cookbook recipe proposal card
description: A recipe-scout proposal card. The `recipe` block is machine-consumed by recipe-scaffold to render the recipe skeleton deterministically; the `context` block is read by recipe-generate's LLM to write the recipe logic and README prose.

name.description: kebab-case recipe name; drives the directory ({name}_python), package (cookbook-{name}-python), and task queue ({name}-task-queue).

provider.description: Zero or more LLM providers; the first drives the scaffolded client + SDK dependency.

title.description: Human title; becomes the README H1.

description.description: One plain sentence; the README front-matter description.

components.description: Optional component names to stub. Defaults are used when omitted.

context.description: Prose/logic inputs the LLM uses in recipe-generate. Not consumed by the scaffolder.

problem.description: What goes wrong without this pattern (2-3 sentences).

source_excerpt.description: 10-25 line excerpt (or pseudocode) showing the pattern.

structure_outline.description: How the recipe is structured: workflow, key activity, tool/API.

closest_recipe.description: Nearest existing recipe and what this adds/changes.

wishlist_gap.description: Coverage-wishlist item this fills, if any.

size_estimate.description: Rough total line count for the finished recipe.

notes.description: Overflow channel: anything load-bearing for building the recipe that does not fit the fields above (gotchas, concurrency or ordering constraints, test strategy). recipe-generate reads these; recurring shapes can be promoted to first-class fields over time.
